title: Sweet Kenyan Fish Curry
description: A nutty and flavorful pescatarian-friendly Kenyan dish featuring coconut milk and fish, perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: Heat the peanut oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the grated ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the curry powder, cumin, and coriander, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the fish fillets and cook until they are browned on both sides and cooked through.
Add the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the curry simmer for 10 minutes.
Melt the dark chocolate in a double boiler or in the microwave in 10 second increments, stirring between each interval.
Temper the chocolate by cooling it to around 82 degrees Fahrenheit, then reheat it to around 90 degrees Fahrenheit.
Stir the tempered chocolate into the curry and serve hot.
